description: Two promises, served one at a time.

hints.text: This is **exportation** (or currying, if you speak functional): $A \land B \to C$ folds into $A \to B \to C$. You're trading a pair of [assumption|assumptions] for a chain of [implication|implications]. Start by assuming $A \land B \to C$.
You're now proving $A \to B \to C$ under that [assumption|assumption]. Assume $A$ — the first link in the chain.
Under $A$, you need $B \to C$. Assume $B$ — the second link.
You have $A$ and $B$ individually. Bundle them into a [conjunction|conjunction] with `and-intro` so you can feed them to your big [assumption|assumption] from step 1.
[modus-ponens|Modus ponens] with $A \land B \to C$ (step 1) and your fresh $A \land B$ (step 4) lands you squarely at $C$.
Finally, [assumption|discharge] the big $A \land B \to C$ to finish. Two promises, delivered one at a time.
